}}This article details the [[Bradford Bulls]] rugby league football club's 2019 season. This is the Bulls 1st season back in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] after gaining promotion from [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] by beating [[Workington Town]] 27–8 in the play off final.

==Season Review==

===August 2018===

It was announced that first team prop forward [[James Davies (rugby league)|James Davies]] left the club to sign for rugby union side Huddersfield RUFC.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.huddersfieldrugby.com/pages/news/story/new-signings-bolster-field-|title=NEW SIGNINGS BOLSTER FIELD|work=Huddersfield Rugby |date=24 August 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> Recruitment for the new season started as on loan prop forward [[Callum Bustin]] signed a permanent contract with the Bulls until the end of 2019.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16351063.bradford-bulls-bustin-signs-deal-to-end-of-2019/|title=Bradford Bulls: Bustin signs deal to end of 2019|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=12 July 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> Bradford brought some experience into the squad with the signing of veteran [[Super League]] centre [[Jake Webster]] from [[Castleford Tigers]] on a 2 Year Deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16589643.bradford-bulls-sign-castleford-centre-jake-webster/|title=Bradford Bulls sign Castleford centre Jake Webster|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=21 August 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> Prop [[James Green (rugby league)|James Green]] who was on loan from [[Castleford Tigers]] signed a 1 Year Deal with the Bulls after a successful loan spell.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16609497.bradford-bulls-loanee-james-green-signs-on-a-one-year-deal/|title=Bradford Bulls loanee James Green signs on a one-year deal|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=30 August 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref>

===September 2018===

Centre [[Ashley Gibson]] signed a 1 Year extension to his contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/51742/gibson-commits-to-the-bulls-for-|title=GIBSON COMMITS TO THE BULLS FOR 2019|work=Bradford Bulls |date=6 September 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> Welsh international winger [[Dalton Grant (rugby league)|Dalton Grant]] also signed a 1 Year extension after scoring 18 tries in the previous season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16859629.winger-dalton-grant-signs-contract-extension-with-bradford-bulls/|title=Winger Dalton Grant signs contract extension with Bradford Bulls|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=13 September 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> It was also announced that former prop forward [[Jon Magrin]] would return to the club after a year with the [[Sheffield Eagles]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16862321.bradford-bulls-re-sign-jon-magrin/|title=Bradford Bulls re-sign Jon Magrin|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=14 September 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> Academy products [[Alix Stephenson]] (fullback), [[Elliot Culling]] (centre) and [[Thomas Doyle (rugby league)|Thomas Doyle]] (hooker) all signed contracts for the first team following in the footsteps of [[Oliver Wilson (rugby league)|Oliver Wilson]], [[Matthew Storton]], [[Rowan Milnes]] and [[Evan Hodgson]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/51791/academy-trio-step-up-to-first-grade|title=ACADEMY TRIO STEP UP TO FIRST GRADE|work=Bradford Bulls |date=18 September 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> Young [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.]] winger [[David Foggin-Johnston]] agreed to sign a 1 Year Deal at the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16893141.bradford-bulls-snap-up-david-foggin-johnston/|title=Bradford Bulls snap up David Foggin-Johnston|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=20 September 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> Home grown fullback [[Brandon Pickersgill]] signed a 2 Year extension to stay at the Bulls until the end of 2020.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16911160.bradford-bulls-brandon-pickersgill-pens-new-two-year-full-time-deal/|title=Bradford Bulls: Brandon Pickersgill pens new two-year full-time deal|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=28 September 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref>

===October 2018===

Young hooker and academy product [[Reiss Butterworth]] signed for [[Super League]] side [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a 3-year deal after not featuring for the Bradford side in 2018.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/45787032|title=Huddersfield Giants sign Bradford Bulls hooker Reiss Butterworth|work=BBC Sport |date=8 October 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> 2018 loanee [[Mikey Wood]] also put pen to paper on a permanent deal as he signed a 1 Year contract to join the Bulls for 2019 from the [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16971811.bradford-bulls-forward-wood-signs-one-year-deal/|title=Bradford Bulls: Forward Wood signs one-year deal|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=9 October 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> Continuing to recruit for the new season the Bulls announced the capture of seasoned hooker/halfback [[Matty Wildie]] on a 1 Year Deal from [[RFL Championship]] side [[Featherstone Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/16980498.bradford-bulls-hooker-wildie-reunited-with-kear/|title=Bradford Bulls: Hooker Wildie reunited with Kear|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=12 October 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> Following this, the Bulls announced that hooker [[Vila Halafihi]] had departed the club later to sign a 1-year deal at [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17196940.bradford-bulls-vila-halafihi-joins-hunslet/|title=Bradford Bulls: Vila Halafihi joins Hunslet|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=2 November 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> Further good news was released by the club as winger [[Jy Hitchcox]] signed a 2 Year Deal with the club from [[Castleford Tigers]] despite interest from [[Toronto Wolfpack]] and [[London Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/51911/bulls-capture-hitchcox-on-two-year-deal|title=BULLS CAPTURE HITCHCOX ON TWO YEAR DEAL|work=Bradford Bulls |date=15 October 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> Bradford re-signed free scoring back rower [[Elliot Minchella]] for 3 years on a full-time deal keeping him at [[Odsal Stadium]] until the end of the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/51926/minchella-signs-full-time-contract-until-end-of-|title=MINCHELLA SIGNS FULL-TIME CONTRACT UNTIL END OF 2021|work=Bradford Bulls |date=18 October 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> Further recruitment in the pack came in the form of Featherstone Rovers second rower [[Connor Farrell]] who signed a 2 Year Deal to play for Bradford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17002620.bradford-bulls-farrell-signs-from-featherstone/|title=Bradford Bulls: Farrell signs from Featherstone|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=23 October 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> Captain and workhorse prop [[Steve Crossley]] signed a new 2 Year Deal with the Bulls.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/51958/crossley-vows-future-with-bradford-to-|title=CROSSLEY VOWS FUTURE WITH BRADFORD TO 2020|work=Bradford Bulls |date=26 October 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> In a double coup for the club [[Joe Keyes (rugby league)|Joe Keyes]] signed a full time 2 Year Deal with the club, whilst out of contract halfback [[Dane Chisholm]] also signed up for 1 Year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/51950/keyes-commits-to-full-time-future-with-the-bulls|title=KEYES COMMITS TO FULL TIME FUTURE WITH THE BULLS|work=Bradford Bulls |date=29 October 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/51964/bulls-re-sign-chisholm-on-one-year-deal|title=BULLS RE-SIGN CHISHOLM ON ONE-YEAR DEAL|work=Bradford Bulls |date=31 October 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref>

===November 2018===

The month started off with the announcement that the Bulls would host [[RFL Championship]] rivals [[York City Knights]] on Boxing Day.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/51978/the-grand-final-that-never-happened---boxing-day-|title=THE GRAND FINAL THAT NEVER HAPPENED - BOXING DAY|work=Bradford Bulls |date=5 November 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> It was also revealed that the Bulls would face [[Super League]] side [[Huddersfield Giants]] on 13 January 2019 for [[Michael Lawrence (rugby league)|Michael Lawrence]]'s testimonial.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.giantsrl.com/news/article/7394/woolford-finalises-pre-season-games|title=WOOLFORD FINALISES PRE-SEASON GAMES|work=Huddersfield Giants |date=5 November 2018 |access-date=9 November 2018}}</ref> The fixtures for the 2019 campaign were also released and Bradford would kick off their return to the Championship with a home match against [[Featherstone Rovers]], it was also revealed that the Bulls would take on local rivals [[Halifax R.L.F.C.]] in the 2019 [[Summer Bash]] at [[Bloomfield Road]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17254830.bulls-begin-championship-with-home-clash-against-featherstone/|title=Bulls begin Championship with home clash against Featherstone|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=25 November 2018 |access-date=1 December 2018}}</ref> Young backrower [[Danny King (rugby league)|Danny King]] left the club and subsequently signed a 1 Year Deal with [[Dewsbury Rams]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/dewsbury-bring-in-bradford-youngster/|title=Dewsbury bring in Bradford youngster|work=Love Rugby League |date=29 November 2018 |access-date=1 December 2018}}</ref> Former player and current [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] assistant coach [[Garreth Carvell]] was brought to the Bulls to continue working under [[John Kear]] as his assistant.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17266894.bradford-bulls-kear-delighted-to-add-gareth-carvell-to-coaching-team/|title=Bradford Bulls: Kear delighted to add Gareth Carvell to coaching team|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=30 November 2018 |access-date=1 December 2018}}</ref> Prop forward [[Jordan Andrade]] also left the Bulls to link up with Dewsbury on a 1 Year Deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://dewsburyrams.co.uk/2018/11/21/rams-sign-andrade/|title=Rams sign Andrade|work=Dewsbury Rams |date=21 November 2018 |access-date=3 December 2018}}</ref>

===December 2018===

December saw the Bulls sign ex [[Super League]] centre [[Rhys Evans (rugby league)|Rhys Evans]] on a 1 Year Deal from [[Leigh Centurions]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17285359.ace-rhys-evans-wings-his-way-into-bradford-bulls/|title=Ace Rhys Evans wings his way into Bradford Bulls|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=8 December 2018 |access-date=8 December 2018}}</ref> Following this capture it was also revealed that the [[RFL Yorkshire Cup|Yorkshire Cup]] would be resurrected after a 26-year absence in the form of a pre-season cup with Bradford, [[Batley Bulldogs]], [[Dewsbury Rams]], [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.]], [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.]], Hunslet Club Parkside and [[York City Knights]] all participating. The Bulls will face Halifax at [[Odsal Stadium]] on 6 January.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/championship-sides-reintroduce-yorkshire-cup-1-9482271|title=Championship sides reintroduce Yorkshire Cup|work=The Yorkshire Post |date=7 December 2018 |access-date=8 December 2018}}</ref> Bradford released their 2019 squad number with fullback [[Gregg McNally]] taking the number 1 shirt and new signings [[Jake Webster]], [[Jy Hitchcox]] and [[Matty Wildie]] taking numbers 3, 5 and 9.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/52121/-squad-numbers-announced|title=2019 SQUAD NUMBERS ANNOUNCED|work=Bradford Bulls |date=15 December 2018 |access-date=15 December 2018}}</ref> During an interview owner Andrew Chalmers revealed that the Bulls would host Canadian side [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in a Friday night friendly on January 25.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17300435.andrew-chalmers-bulls-squad-look-the-part-for-new-season/|title=ANDREW CHALMERS: Bulls squad look the part for new season|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=15 December 2018 |access-date=18 December 2018}}</ref> Young reserve player [[Harvey Burnett]] left the Bulls to join Dewsbury on a 1 Year Deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://dewsburyrams.co.uk/2018/12/11/burnett-arrives-north-at-the-rams/|title=Burnett arrives North at the Rams|work=Dewsbury Rams |date=11 December 2018 |access-date=21 December 2018}}</ref> The Bulls got their pre-season off to a winning 
start with a 20–12 victory over York.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/17320559.bulls-provide-christmas-cheer-with-opening-friendly-win/|title=Bulls provide Christmas cheer with opening friendly win|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=26 December 2018 |access-date=27 December 2018}}</ref>

===January 2019===

The new year started off with the news that fullback [[Gregg McNally]] would be released on compassionate grounds in order to move closer to his home in Leigh to care for his ill wife.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/17331031.bradford-bulls-fullback-wants-to-be-nearer-home-to-care-for-wife/|title=Bradford Bulls: Fullback wants to be nearer home to care for wife|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=2 January 2019 |access-date=2 January 2019}}</ref> The Bulls beat local rivals [[Halifax R.L.F.C.]] 26–16 in the resurrected [[RFL Yorkshire Cup]] meaning they progressed to a semi final against [[Dewsbury Rams]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/17338722.dewsbury-next-for-bulls-after-yorkshire-cup-win-over-halifax/|title=Dewsbury next for Bulls after Yorkshire Cup win over Halifax|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=6 January 2019 |access-date=7 January 2019}}</ref> Following this win Bradford announced that they would face [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in a friendly on the 25 January in the Transatlantic Cup.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/52172/transatlantic-challenge-returns-to-odsal-stadium|title=TRANSATLANTIC CHALLENGE RETURNS TO ODSAL STADIUM|work=Bradford Bulls |date=7 January 2019 |access-date=7 January 2019}}</ref> The Bulls revealed that trialist [[Trae O'Sullivan]] signed a permanent deal with the club after impressing coach [[John Kear]] against Halifax.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/52187/osullivan-signs-on-permanent-deal|title=O'SULLIVAN SIGNS ON PERMANENT DEAL|work=Bradford Bulls |date=11 January 2019 |access-date=12 January 2019}}</ref> The Yorkshire Cup run continued as Bradford defeated Dewsbury 20–18 in the semi-final earning their place in the first Yorkshire Cup final in over 25 years. The following day saw Bradford lose 56–12 to [[Super League]] side [[Huddersfield Giants]] in [[Michael Lawrence (rugby league)|Michael Lawrence]]'s testimonial, halfback [[Dane Chisholm]] scored a length of the field intercept try whilst new signing [[David Foggin-Johnston]] also crossed however the Super League side ran rampant as the Bulls finished the match with most of their Under 19's on the field.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/17354987.giants-far-too-strong-for-bulls-in-lawrence-testimonial/|title=Giants far too strong for Bulls in Lawrence testimonial|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=13 January 2019 |access-date=20 January 2019}}</ref> The Bulls claimed their first piece of silverware for the 2019 season as they won the Yorkshire Cup with a hard-fought 14–12 win against [[Batley Bulldogs]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/17372013.bulls-claim-yorkshire-cup-win-over-batley-bulldogs/|title=Bulls claim Yorkshire Cup win over Batley Bulldogs|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=20 January 2019 |access-date=20 January 2019}}</ref> The month ended with the Bulls losing 48–12 to Toronto in the final pre-season friendly.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/17387697.bradford-bulls-well-beaten-by-toronto-wolfpack/|title=Bradford Bulls well beaten by Toronto Wolfpack|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=26 January 2019 |access-date=4 February 2019}}</ref>

===February 2019===

Bradford opened their 2019 [[Betfred Championship]] campaign with a hard-fought 17–16 win over [[Featherstone Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/match_report/95883|title=Bulls vs. Rovers Match Report|work=Bradford Bulls |date=3 February 2019 |access-date=10 February 2019}}</ref> The Bulls also signed [[Toronto Wolfpack]] prop forward [[Olsi Krasniqi]] on a one-month loan due to an injury crisis in the forward pack.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/17417098.bulls-sign-toronto-wolfpack-ace-olsi-krasniqi-on-loan/|title=Bulls sign Toronto Wolfpack ace Olsi Krasniqi on loan|work=Telegraph & Argus |date=7 February 2019 |access-date=10 February 2019}}</ref> Following their opening round victory the Bulls backed this up with a 31–12 win over [[Swinton Lions]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/match_report/96044|title=Lions vs. Bulls Match Report|work=Bradford Bulls |date=10 February 2019 |access-date=10 February 2019}}</ref> The first loss of the season came in the form of a 24–10 defeat by [[Sheffield Eagles]] with prop forward [[James Green (rugby league)|James Green]] receiving a red card.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/match_report/95893|title=Eagles vs. Bulls Match Report|work=Bradford Bulls |date=17 February 2019 |access-date=10 April 2019}}</ref> The month finished on a sour note for the Bulls as they fell to a 24–14 home defeat by last seasons rivals [[York City Knights]], winger [[Ethan Ryan]] scored a brace of tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/match_report/95902|title=Bulls vs. Knights Match Report|work=Bradford Bulls |date=24 February 2019 |access-date=10 April 2019}}</ref>
